title: penstemon confusus subsp. patens
intro: Penstemon confusus subsp. patens is a plant species in the figwort family, known for its distinctive white or pale purple flowers that bloom from June to September. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Penstemon confusus subsp. patens include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
